title: Draba altaica
taxonomy: Draba altaica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification is part of the genus Draba, which comprises annual or perennial plants often found in alpine or arctic regions. The specific classification of Draba altaica is Draba altaica (Bunge ex Ledeb.) Rupr., indicating its scientific name. Draba altaica is further categorized as a member of the tribe Drabeae, which includes various species of the Draba genus. This classification provides a comprehensive understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Draba altaica has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Some research suggests that Draba altaica may have anti-inflammatory effects, which could be beneficial for treating various conditions. However, more extensive studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant. Further research is necessary to fully understand the potential health benefits of Draba altaica.

Research has suggested that it may have wound-healing and antimicrobial properties, making it a subject of interest in traditional medicine. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. intro: Draba altaica, also known as Altaic whitlowgrass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Draba altaica include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Draba altaica is commonly prepared into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
